reasoning: The FRED data for 1-year real interest rate shows significant volatility with clear mean reversion around a level of ~1.2. Over the last year, whenever the monthly value dropped below 1.0 (which occurred 4 times: April, July, October, December), it subsequently increased in the following month (4 out of 4 times). The current January 2026 value is 0.861, which is below this 1.0 threshold. Although the simple linear model predicts a 35% chance of an increase and the seasonal combined estimate is 51%, the strong tendency for mean reversion from sub-1.0 levels provides compelling evidence for an upward correction by the February 2026 release.
